message.content:
Adversaries may impair defenses by maliciously modifying components within a victim's environment to hinder or disable security mechanisms. This includes both preventative measures like firewalls and anti-virus software, as well as detection capabilities used for auditing and identifying malicious activities. The scope of impairment can affect native defenses and additional security tools installed by users or administrators. Attackers may also target event aggregation and analysis mechanisms, disrupting these processes by altering system components. For instance, Magic Hound has been known to disable LSA protection on compromised hosts using specific registry modifications, while Stuxnet reduces the integrity level of objects to permit unauthorized write actions.

Potential impacts include reduced visibility into malicious activities, delayed detection and response times, and increased risk of data breaches or system compromise. Common adversary tactics involve registry modifications, disabling security services, and altering system configurations.

Mitigation strategies include implementing robust monitoring and alerting systems, regularly auditing and validating security configurations, employing tamper-evident and tamper-resistant technologies, and ensuring that security tools are up-to-date and properly configured to detect and respond to such impairments.
